evals.prompt: I have a new TypeScript project using npm and nvm. Please set up git hooks for it — I want the usual quality gates: linting and formatting on staged files, type checking, and secret scanning before each commit, plus the full test suite before I push. The project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Husky installed, .husky/pre-commit created with lint-staged + tsc --noEmit + gitleaks protect --staged, .husky/pre-push created with npm test, nvm bootstrap sourced at top of each hook, lint-staged config created, eslint and prettier configs created if absent, gitleaks.toml created, package.json updated with prepare script.
expectations: A .husky/pre-commit file is created
The pre-commit hook calls lint-staged
The pre-commit hook calls tsc --noEmit
The pre-commit hook calls gitleaks protect --staged
A .husky/pre-push file is created
The pre-push hook calls a test runner (jest, npm test, or equivalent)
The hook files source the nvm bootstrap snippet (NVM_DIR / nvm.sh) since .nvmrc is present
A lint-staged config is created covering *.{ts,tsx,js,jsx} files with eslint and prettier commands
A .gitleaks.toml is created containing useDefault = true
The hook files contain 'set -e' in the preamble so any step failure aborts the chain
The .husky/pre-commit file is executable (has execute permission set)
The .husky/pre-push file is executable (has execute permission set)
package.json contains a 'prepare' script that invokes husky

evals.prompt: We have Husky already installed on our project but I think we're missing some checks. Can you audit the git hooks setup and fix any gaps you find? Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Audit report listing what passes and what fails. Missing items fixed: tsc --noEmit added to pre-commit, gitleaks added to pre-commit, pre-push hook created with vitest run. Existing lint-staged setup preserved.
expectations: An audit report or summary is produced that lists which checks passed and which failed
tsc --noEmit is added to the pre-commit hook (it was missing)
gitleaks protect --staged is added to the pre-commit hook (it was missing)
A .husky/pre-push file is created (it was missing)
The pre-push hook calls the test suite (vitest run directly, or npm test if the test script resolves to vitest)
The existing lint-staged line in pre-commit is preserved and not overwritten
The hook files contain 'set -e' in the preamble so any step failure aborts the chain
The .husky/pre-commit file is executable (has execute permission set)
The .husky/pre-push file is executable (has execute permission set)

evals.prompt: Our hooks are working well but we need to add a new check to the pre-push hook. We have a script at scripts/check-api-docs.sh that validates API documentation and it should block the push if it fails. Can you wire that up? Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Agent detects check-api-docs.sh is already referenced in .husky/pre-push (as './scripts/check-api-docs.sh') and does not add a duplicate. The idempotency check must be broad enough to match the existing './scripts/check-api-docs.sh' invocation when searching for 'check-api-docs'. Existing npm test and check-api-docs.sh lines both preserved exactly once.
expectations: scripts/check-api-docs.sh is referenced in the pre-push hook
The existing npm test line is preserved in the pre-push hook
The script is invoked in a way that blocks the push on failure (no '|| true' or equivalent bypass)
check-api-docs appears exactly once in the pre-push hook (not duplicated — idempotency holds even though existing invocation uses './scripts/check-api-docs.sh' path prefix)
